commit.message:
v5: multi-pin ADC scan + settle delay to find correct BAT_PIN

BAT_PIN 0 (GPIO0) consistently returns ~285 mV for a 4.163V battery;
expected is ~1040 mV with a 1/4 divider. Root causes identified:

1. Wrong pin: GPIO0 is not the battery divider output. Physical A0 on
   XIAO ESP32-C6 = GPIO2. Changed BAT_PIN to 2 as best guess.

2. No settle delay: large resistor divider presents high impedance to
   the ADC sample-and-hold cap. 10 dummy reads are insufficient; the
   cap needs ~2 seconds to charge, which is why boot screen showed 0.

Changes:
- BAT_PIN: 0 → 2 (GPIO2 = physical A0 on XIAO ESP32-C6)
- Boot screen replaced with 4-pin simultaneous scan (pins 0,1,2,3)
- 2-second settle delay added before scan reads
- "Settling ADC..." message shown immediately so display is not blank
- EMA seeded from post-settle PIN 2 reading (not pre-settle)
- Scan shows all 4 candidates; * marks current BAT_PIN
- User should find pin with ~1040mV reading and update BAT_PIN

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(browser-use): add payment context binding, origin allowlist, and prompt injection scanning (#68)

Browser agents making payments were not bound to origin, session state, or
action descriptions — allowing prompt injections on a page to potentially
smuggle transactions through the tool interface.

This adds three security layers to sardis-browser-use:

1. BrowserPaymentContext: captures origin, page_title, merchant, amount,
   purpose, and session_id at call time, then SHA-256 hashes them into an
   action_hash that travels with the payment to the ledger.

2. Origin allowlist: register_sardis_actions() accepts allowed_origins to
   restrict which page origins may trigger payments.

3. Prompt injection scanning: all string parameters (merchant, purpose,
   origin, page_title) are scanned against the same injection patterns
   used in sardis-api before any payment call reaches the SDK.

Each controller registration gets a unique session_id preventing
cross-session replay.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017ZDeyVY6Ai9ZzQ6fuuUSG7

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v4: restore analogReadResolution(12) and V_DIVIDER=4.0

User measured 4.163V at JST while display showed 0.60V (v3 regression).

Root cause: removing analogReadResolution(12) in v3 left the ADC
un-initialized on this BSP version, causing ~300 mV garbage readings.
The GitHub halving bug (#8999, #11041, #11324) is BSP-version-specific
and does not affect this board. analogReadResolution(12) is required
for correct ADC hardware initialisation here.

Empirical calibration confirmed:
  multimeter 4.163V → ADC ~1040 mV → 1040 × 4.0 / 1000 = 4.16V ✓
  Therefore V_DIVIDER = 4.0 (1/4 resistor divider on Expansion Board v1.1)

Changes:
- Restore analogReadResolution(12) in setup() with detailed warning comment
- Revert V_DIVIDER from 2.0 → 4.0 with empirical justification
- Update readBatRaw() comment to reflect that the call IS required
- Boot screen retained for ongoing calibration verification
- All v3 improvements kept (EMA, LiPo curve, (bool)Serial USB detection)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: redact API keys from exception tracebacks in logs (#283)

PR #264 redacted keys from httpx log messages, but exception
tracebacks (e.g. httpx.HTTPStatusError with ?key=... in the URL)
bypassed the regex because loguru renders {exception} separately.

Add a loguru patcher that sanitizes both message text and exception
args before rendering, covering all output paths (text, JSON, stdlib
intercept). Also apply _redact_sensitive to the JSON sink's
traceback.format_exception output as a defense-in-depth measure.

Fixes #282

commit.message:
fix: use Node 24 and remove registry-url for OIDC publishing

npm OIDC trusted publishing requires npm >= 11.5.1 (ships with
Node 24). Node 22 bundles npm 10.x which lacks OIDC support.
Also remove registry-url from actions/setup-node — it creates a
NODE_AUTH_TOKEN placeholder that overrides the OIDC flow.

Fix AUTH_SECRET: add to Dockerfile build args and DigitalOcean spec

Auth.js v5 requires AUTH_SECRET env var at both build time and runtime.
The deployed app was missing AUTH_SECRET entirely, causing /api/auth/session
to return 500 ("problem with server configuration"). Added ARG/ENV directives
in the builder stage so Next.js can compile auth routes, and updated the
DigitalOcean app spec to include AUTH_SECRET with RUN_AND_BUILD_TIME scope.

commit.message: fix: correct http status attribute references in MCP logs search (#1814) ## Summary Fixes incorrect attribute path references in the MCP logs search filter bar and client-side trace grouping: - **Placeholder text** used `http.status` but the actual OTel attribute is `http.response.status_code` — users would type the placeholder example, get no autocomplete match, and think the feature was broken - **Client-side status extraction** used `http.status_code` instead of `http.response.status_code`, so the `StatusBadge` in trace rows likely never rendered HTTP status codes in the attribute-filtered view ## Test plan - [x] Open MCP logs page, verify placeholder now shows `http.response.status_code != 200` - [x] Type `http.response` in filter bar — autocomplete should show `http.response.status_code` - [x] Apply a `http.response.status_code != 200` filter — verify results filter correctly - [x] Check that trace rows display HTTP status badges when status code data is present Closes AGE-1521 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) --- --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6

fails in CI containers which start fresh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 3: Add clip generation pipeline with highlight detection

New: highlights.js — scoring engine (audio energy 40%, transcript
sentiment 30%, game keywords 20%, pacing 10%). Detects ~30 highlight
segments per 30-min recording, groups into 15-60s clips.

Pipeline handler (pipeline:generateClips) orchestrates: probe source →
create project → extract audio → Whisper transcribe → analyze loudness →
detect highlights → cut clips → thumbnails → save project JSON.

RecordingsView: wired Generate Clips button with real pipeline calls,
progress overlay showing stage/percentage, disabled state during generation.
App.js: loads projects from disk via projectList(), refreshes on generation.

commit.message:
chore: remove duplicate content files from root

Contents are now served from contents/ directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix bullet truncation: disable thinking tokens for STI statement generation

Gemini 2.5 Flash uses thinking by default; without thinking_budget=0 the
thinking pass consumed most of the 4096-token budget, leaving <100 tokens
for the actual bullet text and causing mid-sentence cut-offs.

A single resume bullet needs no chain-of-thought — thinking_budget=0
disables it entirely and 1024 output tokens is ample for 1-2 sentences.
